title: Mellow Smoky Greek Beef Kidney Loukoumades
description: A smoky and crumbly Greek dish featuring beef kidney, perfect for a hangover cure, made with score-fat and cooked in a tandoor-clay-pot.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor-clay-pot to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the sliced beef kidney, score-fat, olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic-free seasoning, salt, and black pepper.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small balls, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter.
Place the balls into the preheated tandoor-clay-pot, leaving about 1 inch of space between each loukoumades.
Cook the loukoumades for 20 minutes, or until they are cooked through and lightly browned on the outside.
Remove the loukoumades from the pot and let them cool for a few minutes.
Serve the loukoumades hot, garnished with fresh parsley or dill if desired.
